title: Can I set up automation for email and activities on subitems?
excerpt:
Hi this feels like a basic question, but will ask anyway as I cant find any info anywhere. Can I set up automation for email and activities on subitems? 
The automation of email & activities on an item is great no issues…

posts.author: AndreCrompton (Andre )
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi this feels like a basic question, but will ask anyway as I cant find any info anywhere. Can I set up automation for email and activities on subitems?

The automation of email & activities on an item is great no issues, but when I try sub items it doesnt seem to trigger, I just want to populate a status on the subitem, when an activity has been added in this case Meeting.

Ive attached the automation im trying to do, I added it in the subitems email & activities automation section, it cam uo with the correct column names on the subitem so i thought i was on a winner, but no.

If you can help or point me to some link that would be great

image1179×384 29.4 KB

posts.author: BruceGosk (Bruce Gosk)
posts.cookedHtml:
I don’t think you can get it working with subitems yet. You may have to use make, or zapier (will cost a couple billable hours probably, unless you do it yourself) to do this assuming you have tried replicating the automation trying to choose the subitem. Looks like a decent feature request also which you should post in that section.

posts.author: sachinkarma (sachin kumar karma)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @AndreCrompton

The challenge with automating emails and activities on subitems in Monday CRM is that standard automation often focuses on main items, while subitems sometimes require additional workarounds. The best approach is to use custom automation recipes or third-party integrations(like Zapier or Make.com) to ensure that subitem status updates when an activity (like a meeting) is added.

  • Check if the trigger applies directly to subitems. If not, try using *parent-item-based triggers and filtering subitems.
  • Utilize Make.comfor advanced logic, linking subitem changes to email and activity updates.
  • If it’s a system limitation, explore webhooks to capture subitem events and trigger necessary updates.
